background.text: Application No. 31915 | Engineering Evaluation | Page 1  of 5

public_notification.text:
Public Notification
n  
Regulation 2-1- 412 
Public notice is required if  an application for an authority to construct or permit to operate for (i) a new or 
modified source located within 1000 feet of the outer boundary of a K- 12 school site and which results in the 
increase in emissions of any substance into the ambient air which has been identified by the California Air 
Resources Board or the APCO as a toxic air contaminant or a hazardous air contaminant or which is on the list 
required to be prepared pursuant to subdivision (a) of Section 25532 or Section 44321 subsections (a) to (f) inclusive of the Health and Safety Code, or (ii) a new or modified source located within an Overburdened 
Community as defined in Sec tion 2 -1-243 and for which a Health Risk Assessment is required pursuant to Section 
2-5-401.  This project is over 1,000 feet from the nearest K- 12 school, and is therefore not subject to the public 
notification requirements for proximity close to a school. The source associated with this application is  located in 
an Overburdened Community; however, there are no new or modified sources for which a Health Risk Assessment 
is required ; therefore, public notice is not triggered  per Regulation 2-1- 412. 
 
Regulation 2-2- 404 
Public notice  is required if an application is for (i ) a new major facility or a major modification of an existing major 
facility, (ii) any new facility, or a modification to any existing facility will involve an increase in emissions in an 
amount that is significant as defined in Section 2-2- 227.2, or (iii) a PSD Project. This application is not a major 
modification of an existing major facility, does not involve an increase in emissions in an amount that is significant, 
and not a PSD project; therefore , public notice is not triggered  per Regulation 2-2- 204.  
 
Regulation 2-4- 405 
Public notice is required for banking applications of any emission reduction  in excess of  40 tons per year of any 
pollutant. This is not a banking application; therefore, public notice is not triggered per Regulation 2-4- 405.  
 Regulation 2-6- 412 
Public notice is required for major facility review permit issuance  of any proposed initial issuance, significant 
revision or five -year renewal of a major facility review permit. This application is not a major facility review 
application; therefore, public notice is not triggered per Regulation 2-6- 412.  
 
8.0 Permit Conditions  
None.  
 
9.0 Title V Permit 
This facility is a major facility that operates pursuant to, and in compliance with, a federal operating permit, aka 
Title V permit, pursuant to Regulation 2, Rule 6, Major Facility Review. Marathon submitted Application No. 30769 
for a significant revision to the Title V permit  as part of the Renewable Fuels Project . Since the proposed ammonia 
injection is part of the same project, Title V changes associated with this permit application will be included in the 
significant revision to the Title V permit via Application No. 30769.   
 
10.0 Recommendation  
I recommend that the Air District issue a Certificate of Exemption  for the following equipment:  
 
S-1601  Aqueous Ammonia Tank T- 895; 300 barrels (Regulation 2 -1-123.2)  
 
 
 
___________________________________________ 
Prepared by:  Danny Nip, Senior Air Quality Engineer  
Date:  11/ 16/2022

raw_sections.preamble: Application No. 31915 | Engineering Evaluation | Page 1  of 5

blocks.heading: ENGINEERING EVALUATION
blocks.text:
ENGINEERING EVALUATION
N  
 
Tesoro Refining & Marketing Company LLC  
150 Solano Way, Martinez, CA 94553  
Plant  No. 14628 
Application No. 31915 
 
This document is the Air District’s Engineering Evaluation Report for the Tesoro Refining and Marketing Company 
LLC application for a Certificate of Exemption for an aqueous ammonia tank, Application No. 31915 . It describes 
how the proposed source satisfie s Air District regulatory requirements for the issuance of a Certificate of 
Exemption.  
 
1.0 - Background  
Tesoro Refining & Marketing Company LLC, an indirect, wholly -owned  subsidiary of Marathon Petroleum 
Corporation (herein referenced as “Marathon”), has applied  for a Certificate of Exemption for the following 
equipment: 
 
S-1601  Aqueous Ammonia Tank T- 895; 300 barrels (Regulation 2 -1-123.2)  
 As part of Marathon’s Renewable Fuels Project (Application No. 30768)  to convert the existing Martinez Refinery 
from a petroleum refinery to an alternative fuels facility, the Air District issued Authorities to Construct to convert 
existing hydrodesulfuriz ation and hydrocracker units to hydrodeoxygenation (HDO) units (S-850, S -1003, and S -
1008 ). The changes covered in the Authorities to Construct include physical modifications of new equipment, new 
reactor catalysts, new fugitive components, and a new sulfiding agent supply vessel. The sulfiding agent will be 
continually injected to sulfide the HDO catalysts in the aforementioned units to keep reactivity.  
 Marathon is proposing to inject aqueous ammonia (NH
4OH) into the three HDO units for corrosion control on an 
as-needed basis  if nitrogen compounds in the renewable feedstocks are at lower concentrations than design 
conditions. The nitrogen compounds are converted to ammonia in the HDO reactors and buffers any carbonic acid 
in the sour water. If there is suf ficient nitrogen in the feed, no ammonia is used. If there is not enough nitrogen, 
the aqueous ammonia is used to control pH and thereby protect the carbon steel of the HDO units.  
 
An existing aqueous ammonia tank for A -1433, F -53 #3 Reformer Feed Preheater SCR Unit, will be reused for the 
ammonia injection. This tank is an existing exempt source without an Air District source number. A source number 
(S-1601) has been assigned and the tank will continue to meet the exemption requirements pursuant to  Regulation 
2-1-123.2, “ Tanks, vessels and pumping equipment used exclusively for the storage or dispensing of any aqueous 
solution which contains less than 1 percent (wt) organic compounds. ” The exemption applies to the storage  
vessel and all associated p iping and components (valves, pumps, and connectors) used for  dispensing aqueous 
ammonia into the system. There are no additional proposed changes to the  process units beyond connecting the 
storage vessel to the feed streams for dispensing aqueous  ammonia.  
 
Application No. 30768 has already evaluated and considered the presence of ammonia generated from nitrogen compounds in the renewable feedstocks , which will end up in the sour water and treated at the Sour Water 
Strippers (S- 1600) . The assumed weight percentage of ammonia in the HDO stream used in Application No. 30768 
is conservative and the facility is not proposing to increase the amount of ammonia in the HDO units as ammonia 
injection will supplement nitrogen loading in the feed to meet the design conditions.  Therefore, there is no change 
to the HDO units (S -850, S -1003, and S -1008)  nor impact to emissions calculation in Application No. 30768 . 
 
Similarly, t he assumed weight percentage of ammonia in the sour water  stream used in Application No . 30768 is 
conservative and the facility is not proposing to increase the amount of ammonia. The sour water strippers (S-
1600) were designed for a maximum ammonia design loading, which will not change with this proposed ammonia 
injection system. The sour w ater strippers will be abated Hydrogen Sulfide (H
2S) Adsorption Vessels (A -2002) and 
Sour Water Stripper Thermal Oxidizer (A -2000). A -2000 will have a minimum ammonia destruction efficiency of 
 
 Application No. 31915 | Engineering Evaluation | Page 2  of 5 
 99.9% (Permit Condition #27591, Part 3) and ammonia emission li mits of 0.11 lbs/hour and/or 963.6 lbs in any 
consecutive 12 month period, measured at the outlet of A -2000 (Permit Condition #27586, Part 4). The ammonia 
emission rates will not change  and the facility will comply with  emission limits established in Appli cation No. 
30768 . 
 
2.0 Emissions Calculation  
S-1601 is a closed tank and the only emissions expected are fugitive emission  from equipment leaks. As the 
components will handle aqueous ammonia, the only air pollutant expected to be emitted is ammonia.  
 
Emissions from equipment leaks at S -1601  were estimated using Marathon- provided component counts and the 
procedures and method ologies outlined in the November 1995 Environmental Protection Agency (EPA) Protocol 
for Equipment Leak Emission Estimates, EPA- 453/R- 95-017. Although the guidelines pertain to equipment leaks of 
total organic compounds , the Air District could not locate any technical documentation stating that the equipment 
leak mechanism (e.g. escaping vapors) of total organic compounds  would differ significantly from that of ammonia. 
However, as the guidelines pertain to equipment leaks of total organic compounds , ammoni a emissions were 
estimated by adjusting the guidelines -listed average emission factors for the difference in molar mass between 
total organic compounds  (assuming a methane  basis) and ammonia and assuming an aqueous ammonia 
concentration of 29.4  percent. The synthetic organic chemical manufacturing industry (SOCMI) emission factors 
were used  since it was considered a better fit for this inorganic material.  
 Marathon provided the component counts shown in Table 1 and the resulting fugitive emissions from existing and 
new fugitive components are estimated in Table 2.  
 
Table 1 – Fugitive Component Counts for Aqueous Ammonia Injection  
  Existing 
Ammonia Tank 
and Injection  
(S-1601)  Dispensing 
to #3 HDO  
(S-850)  Dispensing 
to #2 HDO  
(S-1003)  Dispensing 
to #1 HDO  
(S-1008)  Count Total  
Valves:    
Gas / Vapor  11 0 0 0 11 
Light Liquid  44 24 12 12 92 
Heavy Liquid  0 0 0 0 0 
Equipment:    
Flanges/Connectors  88 45 25 25 183 
PSV's  3 0 0 0 3 
Compressors  0 0 0 0 0 
Pumps (Light Liquids)  2 0 0 0 2 
Pumps (Heavy Liquids)  0 0 0 0 0 
Process Drains  1 0 0 0 1 
Total  149 69 37 37 292 
  
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
  
 
 Application No. 31915 | Engineering Evaluation | Page 3  of 5 
 Table 2 – Ammonia Emissions from  Fugitive Component s 
  Emission Factor1 
(kg/hour/source)  Ammonia 
Emission Factor2 
(lbs/hour/source)  Count 
Total  Emissions 
(lbs/hour)  Emissions 
(lbs/year) 
Valves:   
Gas / Vapor  5.97E -03 4.10E -03 11 4.5E -02 395 
Light Liquid  4.03E -03 2.77E -03 92 2.5E -01 2,232 
Heavy Liquid  2.30E -04 1.58E -04 0 0.0E+00  0 
Equipment:       
Flanges/Connectors  1.83E -03 1.26E -03 183 2.3E -01 2,016 
PSV's  1.04E -01 7.15E -02 3 2.1E -01 1,878 
Compressors  2.28E -01 1.57E -01 0 0.0E+00  0 
Pumps (Light 
Liquids)  1.99E- 02 1.37E- 02 2 2.7E -02 240 
Pumps (Heavy 
Liquids)  8.62E- 03 5.92E- 03 0 0.0E+00 0 
Process Drains  1.70E -03 1.17E -03 1 1.2E -03 10 
Total    292 7.7E -01 6,770  
1EPA Protocol for Equipment Leak Emission Estimates, Table 2 -1 SOCMI Average Emission Factors . 
2Ammonia emission factor = Table 2-1 factor * 2.205 * 17/ 16.04 * 0.294   
(e.g. valve -gas = 5.97E -03*2.205*17/ 16.04 *0.294 = 4.10E -03 lbs/hour/source)  
 
3.0 Cumulative Increase  
There is no increase in emissions under Regulation 2, Rule 2, as a result of this application; therefore, cumulative 
increase does not apply.  
 
4.0 Toxic Screening Analysis  
A Health Risk Assessment (HRA) is required when the emissions of toxic air contaminants (TACs) are equal to or 
exceed the respective trigger levels contained in Regulation 2, Rule 5, Table 2-5-1 ; however, the rule applies to 
new and modified sources of TAC only.  S-1601 meets the exemption requirements of Regulation 2 -1-123.2; 
therefore S -1601 is not subject to Regulation 2, Rule 5. As shown in Table 3, the emissions of TACs do not exceed 
the trigger levels outlined in Regulation 2, Rule 5, Table 2-5-1 ; therefore, S -1601 is an exempt source and does not 
require permitting purs uant to Regulation 2 -1-316.  
 
Table 3 – Fugitive Component Counts for Aqueous Ammonia Injection  
Pollutant   Hourly Emissions  
(lbs/hour)  Annual Emissions 
(lbs/year) Acute  
Trigger Level  
(lbs/hour)  Chronic  
Trigger Level  
(lbs/year) Trigger? 
Ammonia  7.7E -01 6,770  1.4E+00  7.7E+03  No 
 Since the proposed ammonia injection is part of the same project, as defined in Regulation 2-5- 216, as Marathon’s 
Renewable Fuels Project (Application No. 30768) and “in order to discourage circumvention that might  be 
achieved by breaking a project into smaller pieces and submitting more than one  permit application over a period 
of time, a project shall include those new or modified  sources of TACs at a facility that have been permitted within 
the five- year period  immediately preceding the date a complete application is received, and any projects at  that 
facility where an Authority to Construct has been i ssued and has not expired.” Application No. 30768 has been 
reviewed and there is no impact to the HRA conducted as part of that application. S -1601 is an exempt source and  
not subject to Regulation 2, Rule 5.  
 
 
 Application No. 31915 | Engineering Evaluation | Page 4  of 5 
 5.0 Best Available Control Technology (BACT)  
Per Regulation 2 -2-301, BACT is triggered for any new or modified source with the potential to emit 10 pounds or 
more per day of POC, NPOC, NO x, CO, SO 2, PM 10 or PM 2.5 and the modification will result in an increase in emissions 
of that pollutant above baseline levels. S- 1601 is not a new or modified source ; therefore, Regulation 2- 2 and BACT 
do not apply.  
 
6.0 Offsets  
S-1601 is not a new or modified source; therefore, Regulation 2 -2 does not apply and offsets are not required.  
 
7.0 Statement of C ompliance 
Source  S-1601 
Aqueous ammonia storage tank S- 1601  is an existing inorganic storage tank . This tank is  exempt and will continue 
to be exempt from permitting under Sections 2-1- 301 and 2 -1-302 of Regulation 2, Rule 1, Permits Adopted, 
General Requirements due to Section 2-1- 123.2 – tanks, vessels, and pumping equipment used exclusively for the 
storage or dispensing of any aqueous solution that contains less than 1 percent by weight of organic compounds. 
In addition, S -1601  will satisfy the requirements of Regulation 2 -1-319 to qualify for the exemption.  
 
This tank is not subject to Regulation 8,  Rule 5, Organic Compounds – Storage of Organic Liquids , because aqueous 
ammonia is an inorganic liquid. In addition, fugitive components associated with S- 1601 are not subject to 
Regulation 8, Rule 18, Organic Compounds – Equipment Leaks  because these com ponents are used exclusively for 
the dispensing of aqueous ammonia , which is an inorganic liquid. 
 California Environmental Quality Act (CEQA)  
Finding of Exemption:   The Air District has determined that this permit action is exempt from CEQA because the Air District’s approval was 
“ministerial” and therefore exempt from CEQA under CEQA § 21080(b)(1). In addition, the Air District has 
determined that the permit action is also exempt from CEQA per CEQA Guidelines § 15061(b)(3), the “Common Sense” exemption. Furthermore, the Air District has determined that the permit action is categorically exempt 
from CEQA per Air District Regulation 2 -1-312.6, “Permit applications relat ing exclusively to the repair, 
maintenance or minor alteration of existing facilities, equipment or sources involving negligible or no expansion of use beyond that previously existing” and therefore exempt from CEQA under CEQA § 21084; Guidelines § 15301.  
 
Basis for Exemption:   
The Air District’s regulatory requirements that governed the approval of this project involved objective numerical 
standards outlined in the Air District’s Permit Handbook Chapter 3. 4 (Petroleum Refinery Fugitive Emissions), 
which did not allow for or require any subjective judgment or discretion to interpret or apply; and the Air District 
was legally compelled to approve the project where it complied with such standards. Also, the permit application 
did not trigger Best Available C ontrol Technology (BACT) or BACT for toxics. Thus, the Air District’s action was 
ministerial. The applicant has also provided in the permit application CEQA- related information (CEQA Appendix H) 
that demonstrates that the project has no potential for resulting in any additional or different environmental impacts beyond what is already entailed in the applicant’s existing use of the source. Furthermore,  the Air District’s 
permit action is exempt  because  it permits an existing use and does not authorize any expansion of that existing 
use. Source 1601 is an existing aqueous ammonia storage tank for A batement 1433, F -53 #3 Reformer Feed 
Preheater Selective Catalytic Reduction Unit , and will be reused to inject ammonia into the HDO units. There are 
no additional proposed changes to the process units beyond connecting the tank to the feed streams for dispensing the aqueous ammonia. The ammonia emission rates will not change and the facility will comply with 
emissions limits established in Ap plication 30768.      
 
National Emissions Standards for Hazardous Air Pollutants (NESHAP)  
NESHAP is not triggered.  
 
New Source Performance Standards (NSPS)  
NSPS is not triggered.  
 
 Application No. 31915 | Engineering Evaluation | Page 5  of 5 
 Prevention of Significant Deterioration (PSD) 
This application is not part of a PSD project as defined in Regulation 2 -2.

blocks.heading: Public Notification
blocks.text:
Public Notification
n  
Regulation 2-1- 412 
Public notice is required if  an application for an authority to construct or permit to operate for (i) a new or 
modified source located within 1000 feet of the outer boundary of a K- 12 school site and which results in the 
increase in emissions of any substance into the ambient air which has been identified by the California Air 
Resources Board or the APCO as a toxic air contaminant or a hazardous air contaminant or which is on the list 
required to be prepared pursuant to subdivision (a) of Section 25532 or Section 44321 subsections (a) to (f) inclusive of the Health and Safety Code, or (ii) a new or modified source located within an Overburdened 
Community as defined in Sec tion 2 -1-243 and for which a Health Risk Assessment is required pursuant to Section 
2-5-401.  This project is over 1,000 feet from the nearest K- 12 school, and is therefore not subject to the public 
notification requirements for proximity close to a school. The source associated with this application is  located in 
an Overburdened Community; however, there are no new or modified sources for which a Health Risk Assessment 
is required ; therefore, public notice is not triggered  per Regulation 2-1- 412. 
 
Regulation 2-2- 404 
Public notice  is required if an application is for (i ) a new major facility or a major modification of an existing major 
facility, (ii) any new facility, or a modification to any existing facility will involve an increase in emissions in an 
amount that is significant as defined in Section 2-2- 227.2, or (iii) a PSD Project. This application is not a major 
modification of an existing major facility, does not involve an increase in emissions in an amount that is significant, 
and not a PSD project; therefore , public notice is not triggered  per Regulation 2-2- 204.  
 
Regulation 2-4- 405 
Public notice is required for banking applications of any emission reduction  in excess of  40 tons per year of any 
pollutant. This is not a banking application; therefore, public notice is not triggered per Regulation 2-4- 405.  
 Regulation 2-6- 412 
Public notice is required for major facility review permit issuance  of any proposed initial issuance, significant 
revision or five -year renewal of a major facility review permit. This application is not a major facility review 
application; therefore, public notice is not triggered per Regulation 2-6- 412.  
 
8.0 Permit Conditions  
None.  
 
9.0 Title V Permit 
This facility is a major facility that operates pursuant to, and in compliance with, a federal operating permit, aka 
Title V permit, pursuant to Regulation 2, Rule 6, Major Facility Review. Marathon submitted Application No. 30769 
for a significant revision to the Title V permit  as part of the Renewable Fuels Project . Since the proposed ammonia 
injection is part of the same project, Title V changes associated with this permit application will be included in the 
significant revision to the Title V permit via Application No. 30769.   
 
10.0 Recommendation  
I recommend that the Air District issue a Certificate of Exemption  for the following equipment:  
 
S-1601  Aqueous Ammonia Tank T- 895; 300 barrels (Regulation 2 -1-123.2)

Date:  11/ 16/2022

 5.0 Best Available Control Technology (BACT)  
Per Regulation 2 -2-301, BACT is triggered for any new or modified source with the potential to emit 10 pounds or 
more per day of POC, NPOC, NO x, CO, SO 2, PM 10 or PM 2.5 and the modification will result in an increase in emissions 
of that pollutant above baseline levels. S- 1601 is not a new or modified source ; therefore, Regulation 2- 2 and BACT 
do not apply.  
 
6.0 Offsets  
S-1601 is not a new or modified source; therefore, Regulation 2 -2 does not apply and offsets are not required.  
 
7.0 Statement of C ompliance 
Source  S-1601 
Aqueous ammonia storage tank S- 1601  is an existing inorganic storage tank . This tank is  exempt and will continue 
to be exempt from permitting under Sections 2-1- 301 and 2 -1-302 of Regulation 2, Rule 1, Permits Adopted, 
General Requirements due to Section 2-1- 123.2 – tanks, vessels, and pumping equipment used exclusively for the 
storage or dispensing of any aqueous solution that contains less than 1 percent by weight of organic compounds. 
In addition, S -1601  will satisfy the requirements of Regulation 2 -1-319 to qualify for the exemption.  
 
This tank is not subject to Regulation 8,  Rule 5, Organic Compounds – Storage of Organic Liquids , because aqueous 
ammonia is an inorganic liquid. In addition, fugitive components associated with S- 1601 are not subject to 
Regulation 8, Rule 18, Organic Compounds – Equipment Leaks  because these com ponents are used exclusively for 
the dispensing of aqueous ammonia , which is an inorganic liquid. 
 California Environmental Quality Act (CEQA)  
Finding of Exemption:   The Air District has determined that this permit action is exempt from CEQA because the Air District’s approval was 
“ministerial” and therefore exempt from CEQA under CEQA § 21080(b)(1). In addition, the Air District has 
determined that the permit action is also exempt from CEQA per CEQA Guidelines § 15061(b)(3), the “Common Sense” exemption. Furthermore, the Air District has determined that the permit action is categorically exempt 
from CEQA per Air District Regulation 2 -1-312.6, “Permit applications relat ing exclusively to the repair, 
maintenance or minor alteration of existing facilities, equipment or sources involving negligible or no expansion of use beyond that previously existing” and therefore exempt from CEQA under CEQA § 21084; Guidelines § 15301.  
 
Basis for Exemption:   
The Air District’s regulatory requirements that governed the approval of this project involved objective numerical 
standards outlined in the Air District’s Permit Handbook Chapter 3. 4 (Petroleum Refinery Fugitive Emissions), 
which did not allow for or require any subjective judgment or discretion to interpret or apply; and the Air District 
was legally compelled to approve the project where it complied with such standards. Also, the permit application 
did not trigger Best Available C ontrol Technology (BACT) or BACT for toxics. Thus, the Air District’s action was 
ministerial. The applicant has also provided in the permit application CEQA- related information (CEQA Appendix H) 
that demonstrates that the project has no potential for resulting in any additional or different environmental impacts beyond what is already entailed in the applicant’s existing use of the source. Furthermore,  the Air District’s 
permit action is exempt  because  it permits an existing use and does not authorize any expansion of that existing 
use. Source 1601 is an existing aqueous ammonia storage tank for A batement 1433, F -53 #3 Reformer Feed 
Preheater Selective Catalytic Reduction Unit , and will be reused to inject ammonia into the HDO units. There are 
no additional proposed changes to the process units beyond connecting the tank to the feed streams for dispensing the aqueous ammonia. The ammonia emission rates will not change and the facility will comply with 
emissions limits established in Ap plication 30768.      
 
National Emissions Standards for Hazardous Air Pollutants (NESHAP)  
NESHAP is not triggered.  
 
New Source Performance Standards (NSPS)  
NSPS is not triggered.
